    • Simulated zero inflated Beta distribution example

    Parallelized normalised predictive density over vector z

    ZIB_convolution.Rd

    Parallelized normalised predictive density over vector z

    ZIB_convolution(
  z_values,
  alpha_input,
  beta_input,
  zi_input,
  weighted_samps,
  weights,
  point
)

    Arguments

    z_values

    evaluation points

    alpha_input

    Point estimates (point=TRUE) or matrix (point=FALSE) of shape parameters for each element (b) in the aggregate over each of the N observations.

    beta_input

    Point estimates (point=TRUE) or matrix (point=FALSE) of shape parameters for each element in the aggregate.

    zi_input

    Point estimates (point=TRUE) or matrix (point=FALSE) of zero-inflation probability parameters for each element in the aggregate.

    weighted_samps

    Array of weighted samples for each element in the aggregate (dimensions: n_sims x n_draws x b).

    weights

    vector of weights that are used to combine to form the aggregated density Z. (length b)

    point

    A true/false indicator to denote whether you are using point estimates (point=TRUE) or posterior samples (point=FALSE) of the Beta parameters.

    Value

    The aggregate density Z over a grid of values using a convolution of zero-inflated Beta distributions.

    Details

    A wrapper function for calculating Monte Carlo estimates for the aggregate density Z using the zero-inflated Beta distribution.

    Contents

    Developed by Hannah Comiskey, David T. Frazier, Ole Maneesoonthorn.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
